zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Holland I於1325年出生在英國藍卡爾普雷斯特維其時，他的父親Thurston de Holland II35歲，母親Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire30歲。他於大約1355年與Ameria de Kenyon在英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯特維其結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1402年7月1日在他的家鄉享年77歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯特維其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Holland I于1325年出生在英国藍卡爾普雷斯特維其时，他的父亲Thurston de Holland II35岁，母亲Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire30岁。他于大约1355年与Ameria de Kenyon在英国英格兰藍卡爾普雷斯特維其结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1402年7月1日在他的家乡享年77岁，埋葬在英国英格兰藍卡爾普雷斯特維其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Holland I was born in 1325, in Prestwich, Lancashire, England, his father, Thurston de Holland II, was 35 and his mother, Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire, was 30. He married Ameria de Kenyon about 1355, in Prestwich, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 1 July 1402, in his hometown, at the age of 77, and was buried in Prestwich, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Holland I est né en 1325, à Prestwich, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Thurston de Holland II, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Ameria de Kenyon environ 1355, à Prestwich, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 1 juillet 1402, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à Prestwich, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Holland I 1325 in Prestwich, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thurston de Holland II 35 und seine Mutter Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire 30. Er hat um 1355 in Prestwich, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Ameria de Kenyon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 1. Juli 1402 im Alter von 77 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Prestwich, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Holland I è nato il nel 1325 nel Prestwich, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Thurston de Holland II, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Ameria de Kenyon circa 1355, nel Prestwich, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 1 luglio 1402, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolto a Prestwich, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Holland Iが1325年にイギリスランカシュアイアプレストウィチで生まれた時，父Thurston de Holland IIは35歳，母Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashireは30歳であった。約1355年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアプレストウィチでAmeria de Kenyonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1402年7月1日に故郷で77歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアプレストウィチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Holland I(이)가 1325년 영국 랭카실르 플레스트위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thurston de Holland II(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1355년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 플레스트위치에서 Ameria de Kenyon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1402년 7월 1일 그의 고향에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 플레스트위치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Holland I nasceu em 1325, em Prestwich, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thurston de Holland II, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Ameria de Kenyon aproximadamente 1355, em Prestwich, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1 de julho de 1402, em sua cidade natal, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em Prestwich, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Holland I родился в 1325 г. в местности прествич, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thurston de Holland II было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Ameria de Kenyon около 1355 г. в местности прествич, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 1 июля 1402 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности прествич, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Holland I nació en 1325, en Prestwich, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thurston de Holland II, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Lady Mary De Collyer of Lancashire, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Ameria de Kenyon aproximadamente 1355, en Prestwich, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 1 de julio de 1402, en su pueblo, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultado en Prestwich, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
